pattern: Greedy selection of the largest Fibonacci number not exceeding the remainder, repeated until zero, always yields the minimum count.
hints: Precompute all Fibonacci numbers up to k; there are at most ~45 of them below 10^9.
Greedily subtract the largest Fibonacci number that fits into the remaining value.
Each greedy step reduces k to a strictly smaller non-negative number, so the loop terminates.

edge_inputs.description: k is itself a Fibonacci number
k=2 which is a Fibonacci number itself

stress.description: random k values across full range
